sound_type: infrasound at 10-40 Hz, below human hearing. 188 dB -- loudest animal sound. Calls travel hundreds of miles via SOFAR channel. E=5
ecosystem: Open ocean, deep water. Rare visitor to Washington outer coast. Continental shelf edge. Acoustic detections only.
season: Year-round in eastern North Pacific. Seasonal movements following krill concentrations.
family: Balaenopteridae (second entry -- Humpback Whale Song at 191)

coordinates.description: Blue Whale off Washington outer coast, continental shelf edge. Deep ocean. Acoustic detection only.

taxonomy_note.description: Second Balaenopteridae (after Humpback Whale Song 191). THE LARGEST ANIMAL THAT HAS EVER LIVED. 188 dB -- loudest animal sound. Infrasound below hearing. 15 consecutive marine mammals (180-194).
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Balaenopteridae: Minke Whale (190), Humpback Whale Song (191), Blue Whale Infrasound (194).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Balaenoptera from Greek 'balaena' (whale) + 'pteron' (fin/wing). musculus from Latin 'musculus' (little mouse, also muscle) -- a Linnaean joke. 'Blue Whale' for its blue-gray coloring underwater.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Balaenoptera -- 8 species including Blue, Fin, Sei, Bryde's, Minke, Humpback.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Cetacea. Balaenopteridae third entry overall in SPS.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Seventh marine mammal family (shared): Phocidae, Otariidae, Mustelidae, Phocoenidae, Delphinidae, Balaenopteridae, Eschrichtiidae.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Marine mammal era: 15 consecutive (180-194). Approaching Anatidae record of 17.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 10-90 Hz. A/B calls: 10-20 Hz. D calls: 25-90 Hz.
song_metrics.peak_energy_band_hz: 15-20 Hz (A calls -- the 'classic' blue whale call).
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: A calls: 15-20 Hz. B calls: 10-18 Hz. D calls: 25-90 Hz.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: A calls: 15-20 sec. B calls: 1-5 sec. D calls: 1-4 sec.
song_metrics.note_structure: A calls (long moans), B calls (pulses), D calls (downswept). A and B alternate in predictable patterns.
song_metrics.note_types: Long moans (A), pulses (B), downswept arch sounds (D). All infrasonic or near-infrasonic.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: A-B alternation patterns. Songs can last hours. Regular temporal structure.
song_metrics.timbre: Below hearing for most content. If pitch-shifted: deep, resonant, haunting moans. Steady pitch. Enormous.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=5. 188 dB re 1 uPa at 1 m. LOUDEST ANIMAL SOUND. But below human hearing. Paradox of inaudibility.
song_metrics.tempo: Slow. 15-20 second calls with pauses. Deliberate. Matches the scale of the animal.
song_metrics.context: Open ocean, deep water. Communication across ocean basins. Mating calls. Population monitoring via acoustic arrays.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Blue whale call frequencies have been declining worldwide over decades (McDonald et al. 2006). Reason unknown.
song_metrics.key_property: INFRASOUND. 188 dB. LOUDEST ANIMAL. LARGEST ANIMAL EVER. Below hearing. SOFAR channel. 500-1,600 km range. E=5. v1.49.335.

pelage.overall: Blue-gray dorsal surface. Mottled with lighter patches. Yellowish ventral surface (from diatom accumulation). Small dorsal fin far back on body.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Enormous size (largest animal). Blue-gray coloring. Tiny dorsal fin relative to body. Mottled. Tall columnar blow (9 m / 30 ft).
pelage.seasonal_variation: Diatom accumulation varies. Otherwise consistent year-round.
pelage.swimming_pattern: Slow cruiser (5-8 mph). Can sprint to 20 mph. Deep diver (to 200 m). Solitary or small groups (1-3). Not acrobatic.

physical_description.overall: Length: up to 30 m (98 ft). Weight: up to 200 tons. LARGEST ANIMAL EVER. Heart: size of VW Beetle. Tongue: weight of elephant. Aorta: child could crawl through. Baleen plates.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Sheer size. Nothing else is this large. Blue-gray. Tiny dorsal fin. Columnar blow. Mottled lighter patches.
physical_description.field_id: Extremely rare visual sighting off Washington coast. Enormous blue-gray whale. Columnar blow to 9 m. Tiny dorsal fin. Most detections are acoustic only via hydrophone arrays.

nodes.title: The Absolute Superlative -- Largest Animal Ever
nodes.description: 200 tons. 30 meters. 188 dB. No larger animal in 4.5 billion years of Earth. The absolute maximum of biological scale.

nodes.title: Infrasound -- Below Human Hearing
nodes.description: 10-40 Hz calls below the ~20 Hz hearing threshold. SOFAR channel propagation. 500-1,600 km range. Ocean basin communication.

nodes.title: Paradox of Inaudibility -- 188 dB = Silence
nodes.description: The loudest animal sound on Earth is below human hearing. 188 dB = silence to us. E=5 reflects power without audibility.

nodes.title: 15 Consecutive Marine Mammals
nodes.description: Degrees 180-194. 15 entries. 7 families. E=1 to E=7. Approaching Anatidae record of 17.

cross_references.relationship: BALAENOPTERIDAE PAIR! Humpback (E=6, audible song) vs. Blue Whale (E=5, inaudible infrasound). Same family, opposite audibility.
LOUDEST EVENT! Breach E=7 (audible) vs. Blue Whale E=5 (inaudible). Audibility inverts amplitude.
SILENCE BOOKENDS! Porpoise E=1 (true silence) vs. Blue Whale E=5 (inaudible power). Both unheard. Different reasons.
EXTREMES OF SCALE! Twee pop E=3 (smallest genre) + Blue Whale (largest animal ever). Both easily overlooked.
